name: Comma v0.1-2T
archDisplay: Dense decoder-only transformer, Llama 3 architecture, 7B, trained with 4096-token sequences
note: 7B model trained for 2T tokens (1.93T main phase + 75.5B cool-down) entirely on the openly-licensed/public-domain 'Common Pile v0.1' dataset (8TB), demonstrating that legally clean training data can rival models trained on unlicensed web text; a 1T-token sibling checkpoint (comma-v0.1-1t) was released alongside it.
verificationNotes: Confirmed via TechCrunch's June 6, 2025 report, EleutherAI's own blog post (blog.eleuther.ai/common-pile), and the common-pile/comma-v0.1-2t Hugging Face model card, which gives the full benchmark table used below. Training used length-4096 sequences per the Common Pile arXiv paper (2506.05209) — used as ctxK rather than an unverified secondary claim of a 128K window, which looked inconsistent with a from-scratch 7B trained on only 2T tokens with no stated long-context extension stage. This fills the task's explicit instruction to check EleutherAI for missed releases; the corpus's eleutherai folder currently only has GPT-J/GPT-NeoX/GPT-Neo/Pythia (2021-2023), so this 2025 release is a genuine gap.
